[[Category:grammatical numbers]]
The '''paucal''' is a grammatical number in [[Aelaki]] used to express a small quantity -- "a few" of something.

== Formation ==

The paucal is expressed through the [[Inanimate|inanimate]] [[Ki_syllables|Ki]] marker '''ïf''', which indicates a small quantity of mass/uncountable nouns.

== Restriction to inanimate nouns ==

The paucal and [[Plentiful|plentiful]] are '''exclusive to [[Inanimate|inanimate]] (mass/uncountable) nouns'''. Animate nouns ([[Child|child]], [[Female|female]], [[Male|male]] genders) do not have paucal or plentiful forms — to express "a few" or "many" with animate nouns, speakers use quantity words instead.

== Relationship to Other Numbers ==

The paucal sits between the [[Aelaki plural|plural]] (partitive "some of") and the [[Singular|singular]] (one) in terms of quantity. It is paired with the [[Plentiful|plentiful]], which similarly expresses quantity but indicates abundance rather than scarcity.

{| class="wikitable"
! Number !! Quantity !! Morphological Strategy
|-
| [[Singular]] || one || Base template (unmarked)
|-
| '''Paucal''' || a few || Inanimate plural marker (ïfïf)
|-
| [[Aelaki plural|Plural]] || some of || Reduplication of C₂
|-
| [[Plentiful]] || many || Abundance marker
|-
| [[Collective]] || all || Umlaut (vowel fronting)
|-
| [[Zero]] || none || Zero-infix /f/
|}

== See Also ==

* [[Aelaki plural|Plural]]
* [[Plentiful]]
* [[Singular]]
* [[Collective]]
* [[Zero]]
